=s.text:
v.01

Differentiated the console and user's messages by colors and symbols (|, >)

Added the ability to create files, nested directories kinda work a little bit. Only a little bit, the rest is just barbeque sauce.

Added commands:

- ls
- nf

Fixed a bug where the terminal would stop printing completely after clearing. (cls)

Officially declared script files to be .lime files.

What is Lime?

Lime is the scripting language used by Line.

You are “using Lime” only when working with .lime files. Creating them, editing them, or executing them. 

Lime is responsible for script logic and behavior, not for general terminal commands.


Typing commands like cd, ls, or print is not Lime.

Printing a .txt file is not Lime.

Those are Line operations.

Lime is strictly involved in the creation and execution of .lime scripts.

=t.text:
Configuration for https://turbowarp.org/
You can move, resize, and minimize this comment, but don't edit it by hand. This comment can be deleted to remove the stored settings.
{"hq":true} // _twconfig_

=u.text:
v.02

When a line is added with more than 39 characters, it accounts for wrapping by adding an invisible spot that prevents the terminal from writing on the same line twice.
This setting is called "WrapFactoring", and is enabled by default in TOGS

You can now disable PTE++ rendering of the terminal.
It is a setting in TOGS called "renderPTE++" which is enabled by default.

If you do "ls" in a directory that has a nested directory in it, only the name of the nested directory will be read, not the entire contents of it.

The symbols that are added automatically at the start of every message in the terminal now have a TOGS switch.
It's known as "LinePrefix", and is enabled by default in TOGS, as nature intended.

You can now make the "ls" command read you EVERYTHING inside of a directory by disabling "cleanLS" in TOGS

You can finally interact with SCS and TOGS through the terminal! 
The commands are scs:dev and togs:dev
I will post a section about how to use them in the wiki at some point... hi github!

Redid the ls command 3 times, added the message que.

Reworked the entire command reading script. It's more customizable and easier to change now. It can now detect if a command exists or doesnt exist! It couldnt before... that was a problem.

You can now change the font by messing with the "font:" in TOGS.
Fonts included in PTE++:
Curvature
Parabolic
Geometric
Angular

If PTE++ does not detect a font, it automatically sets the font to angular and sends a warning message.

Created LIMB. the Line Immediate Memory Batch. Which serves as the memory batch for line immediately at the command of the user or Line/Lime operations.
It wont become useful until i get into .lime files and apps, so for now, its just a really big heap of barbeque sauce.

"SimpleNewLine" in TOGS has been renamed "OldNewLine", but it is still enabled by default because of my muscle memory and accessibility purposes. I want to be able to use this on my ipad.
Due to this change, you can now press Enter to start a new line instead of pressing - and = at the same time to make a new line.

You can now replace items in SCS and TOGS through:

scs/togs:dev -re -dumbsetting -smartsetting

^ this line above will replace the setting called "dumbsetting" with the user specified "smartsetting", Line WILL NOT check if the new setting will actually do something.

I fixed a problem that showed up after messing with TOGS, but i dont know why the problem showed up? I dont know why it didnt show up BEFORE i edited TOGS through the terminal... it's fixed now though, I just thought it was weird.
Dont mess with TOGS, kids.

For the sake of infinite outputs, LIMB is fully functional now. At the cost of my sleep.
In other words: "I eated all the bbq sauce"

Improved wrap factoring calculation

Added the Rep/Repeat command. It repeats your last command. If the last command was rep, it will just do the command that was sent before that one, and so on.

Added the Uptime command

If i forgot something, check help for commands.

=v.text:
v.03/04

--- COMMANDS ---

 - Added the Del/Delete command.
 
 - Added the lde command, it prints the entirety of all the directories. If the -el flag is added to the command, it will exclude the Line directory from being printed. the lde command is in the place of a previously added command called ls_deep. If the -ln flag is added, line numbers will be put on the beginning to each line.

- CLS with the flag -deep now deletes ALL of the directories if AdvancedOn is enabled.

 - If you press R when not in a textbox, the terminal will re-render.

 - If you press ; and r at the same time, it will refresh SCS.

- The tick command changes the contents of .cset files by exactly 0.02

- Added the TxtSize command, which changes the text size. "It's pretty cool." -Splotchum

- Added the execute command, which executes lime scripts.

Tl;dr:

- cls -deep
- "R"
- ";" + "R"
- tick
- txtSize
- exe


--- TECHNICAL CHANGES ---

 - Replaced the "Pluh" error message with "Current directory is corrupted"

 - Making a text file now requires you to use quotation marks around the contents.

 - Internal Development: Now, every time i make a significant change within an unreleased verison, i will change the internal version number by 0.01.

 - The Line directory and environment is now hosted inside of the {main} directory.

 - Limb folders are fully functional

 - 


Splotchum Fixes:

 - When a message sent by the system is surrounded in hard brackets, the color is changed to cyan
 - When changing directories, if the directory the user is asking for doesnt not exist, the system will try to add the name of the current directory to the requested directory before sending an error message. We need this because of situations like when the directory is displayed as {dir2}, but it is named by the system to be {dir1}:{dir2} via nesting.
 - Miscellaneous fixes relating to optimization, this thing now runs on an ipad.

=w.text:
"> Welcome to Line!" This is a personal project made by Min Fou, started in January of 2026.
The goal of the Line project is explained by this quote: "If you don't know how a boat floats, build a boat. Then you will know how a boat floats.", meaning that since I dont know how an OS works, I will go headfirst into building my own, even if it is in the most simple programming language that exists.

I am very proud of my work here, and I am not really sure how to show it off, so I guess if you are reading this, you are one in a million!

Why i dont use AI: 
If I tell AI to create something, I can never live down the fact that It wasnt me that created it. I want to be proud of something that i made on my own, with my own ideas, my own skills, and my own experience.
I just want a more true feeling project here. I dont care if anybody ever reads this.

=x.text:
Roadmap

[x] Create .tbl and .cfg files 
[ ] Allow editing of .tbl
[ ] Allow editing of .cfg files
[x] Create functional environments 
[ ] Create an environment that acts as a text editor for .tbl, .txt, and .cfg files.
[x] Include additional metadata flags for files, and metadata .cfg files for directories.
[ ] Make packages work
[ ] It would be really cool if the entirety of an environment's messages were compressed into a .tbl file when you leave, and uncompressed when you enter it. But an environment should have a rule in it's config file that makes it so it stays "awake" even when you leave it.

- Version 5 will just be getting the Environment features OUT THERE, and then 06 will be polishing and bugfixes.

=y.text:
v.05

--- New Stuff ---

- Revamped LIMB entirely.

- There is now a little logo printed in the bottom right of the screen that reflects which environment the user is currently in. The Line logo for {line}, a < bracket for {main}, and a lime for {lime}.

- started the long road of creating Packages, there's only one right now.

- added the pkg command which empties the contents of .pkg files into a specified directory. 

- SCS is now visual for a split second on startup. This is tied to the control set "SCS_Progress"

- SCS set "Initialize_LEnv" has been replaced with "Initialize_LDefaults"

- Created filetype .tbl
- Created filetype .cfg

- Reworked the ral command to actually do something, it was entirely nuked after making all of the Environment changes. ral now has an integer parameter that allows you to specify how many lines from the history it should reprint. "ral -1" prints the last message sent, whether sent by the system or the user. 

- Revamped the wrapping system, dont push it though. Disable it by removing "TorchWrapping" in TOGS

--- Environment Changes ---

Migrated Line to the Environment's line adder.

ral now reprints the current environment's contents rather than just Line's



--- Misc. Changes ---

Deleting directories used to only delete the first item, now it deletes the entire directory with all it's contents. 

Fixed a bug where commands would be indefinitely displayed as the newest message even if the system printed output.

Added the rand script command, to generate 7 random numbers from between 3 to 12, it would look like: rand:3:12:7

Added the printLimb script command. To print out the most recent limb: printlimb:rec, to print out a specific one: printlimb:13 / printlimb:(any num)

Fixed a bug where if you added "LoadAll" to SCS, it would just instantly reload SCS from memory, deleting the LoadAll command, meaning that nothing else would load.

--- Splotchum Fixes ---

"I just kept on making changes, and it kept on working! It was insane." -Splotchum
"TO WIN THE GAME." -WOPR
"Stupid like... build a computer that knows absolutely everything, and then lose control of it?" -Max Peterson, Echelon Conspiracy

--- ROADMAP ---
_Things i wish i did_


Roadmap

- Create .tbl and .cfg files 
- Allow editing of .tbl
- Allow editing of .cfg files
- Create functional environments 
- Create an environment that acts as a text editor for .tbl, .txt, and .cfg files.
- Include additional metadata flags for files, and metadata .cfg files for directories.
- Make packages work
- It would be really cool if the entirety of an environment's messages were compressed into a .tbl file when you leave, and uncompressed when you enter it.

- Version 5 will just be getting the Environment features OUT THERE, and then 06 will be polishing and bugfixes.

=z.text:
v.06

--- CORE CHANGES ---
_The most important changes_


# 1Migrated storage from being stored in Directories to being stored in [STORAGE]

# 2 Decided Most of the Lime functions were useless so i deleted them in favor of new, renamed Citrus functions and commands.

# 3 Migrated directories to become purely path-based. 

# 4 Created--but not implemented--the variable system.

# 5 Modified the pointer system to not need splitting, only comparisons.

# 6 Every message sent to the current directory is now stored as a .txt file.

# 7 Implemented tags: Environment Identifier: [#*Ees], Directory Identifier: [T*taG]

# 8 Migrated from paths to a six-lane storage system that has 6 separate lists to store data in. This makes the system much faster when searching for, and editing files. I call it "Pathsx6". The tags made in Core Change #7 have been replaced with simply "Directory" and "Environment", and are stored in the "types" list.

# 9 Fully implemented the variables system.

--- Changes ---

#1 - The cd variable has been renamed to cwd

#2 - Wonda Val's name has been changed to "Mr. iCMD", the variable "iCMD" is an extremely frequently used (54 usages as of writing this) variable that serves as the output to many functions.

#3 - Changed the Line sprite logo to a little computer guy (Its april 1st yaay)

#4 - Changed the Eek logo to a little rainbow thingy

#5 - For some reason function nesting with screen refresh turned off is really really laggy, so I modified the offending scripts, and now Line releases perform well off the bat.

#6 - Removed various redundant scripts and branches

#7 - Fixed a bug where ral would only print every other line, so now it prints every line you tell it to, no skipping.

#8 - Removed the error message "! Error, file or directory exists outside of {Main}" due to it becoming redundant.

#9 - Modified the nf and cd commands to be compatible with Paths

#10 - Modified the "Message of the day" package to be consistent with Paths.

#11 - Modified "ral" to work with Paths

#12 - Modified the rep/repeat command to both be actually functional once again and work with Paths. The rep command also only repeats the most recent SUCCESSFUL command, so if you made a syntax error or misspelled the command, it wont repeat those.

#13 - The cat command has been modified to work with Paths

#14 - Added feedback for creating a file.

#15 - Text is now scaled properly and takes up the entire screen no matter how small it is. 

#16 - Changed the delete command to delete files using their names instead of numbers.

#17 - Corrected the logos to show up when you are in a directory.

#18 -  Added "DrawBG" to TOGS, which allows you to control whether the background color gets drawn or not.

#19 - Added "DrawLogos" to TOGS, which allows you to control if the logos get drawn or not.

#20 - Removed the three "w" commands and the "print18" commands due to them becoming useless.

#21 - Went through ALL of the previous fixes that were made compatible with paths, and made them compatible with pathsx6

#22 - Added the fd command which allows you to view the names or contents of every file in any directory.

#23 - Created the first 2 config files in the system folder, syscolor.cfg controlling the color of the background (as a hex code set to 000000), and the sysfontsize.cfg file controlling the size of the text (set to 12 by default).

#24 - Functions like "Copy", "move", and "Delete" all use recursive functionality to effect entire directories and every single branch of every single directory in there.

#25 - Fixed a bug where you could make a file that has no name by entering it's name as a pipe.

--- Splotchum Fixes ---
Splotchum was off duty this week due to spring break
"Dogs make some weird noises. Noises that would immediately make us disgusted if they came from any other animal. Most of us just ignore the sounds dogs make."

--- Known Issues :( ---
Using ral right after boot doesnt give you the entire SCS logs. It's weird and i dont understand it. This is 100% a job for splotchums. (Fixed by Change # 7)
Eek kinda sucks right now
"cls -deep" deletes everything. EVERYTHING.

--- Notes ---

You have no idea how good it feels to see my system boot after an hour of migrating the files, I have never been so happy to see 0 error messages and the text "> Welcome to Line!" on my monitor.

The error message i will always hate the most is ral's "[Something went wrong]" because back in v05, that meant that your entire system was just broken and there was nothing you could ever do again without restarting the entire thing.

If you are a cool kid and use turbowarp's high quality pen like me i recommend using font size 4

I admit i wish i instead went object oriented instead of everything being a path. I watched a couple videos on the "Big O" rating system, and i have some notes.

"Everything is a thing." -Milo

"It would be so cool if you let me use profanity in the changelog" -Milo

Okay, now storage is stored in 6 lists and everything is really really fast and simple to access, you can find the contents of any file you want just by using two default blocks! Compare that to the multiple functions you had to use just to get a single piece of info from a file. This system is extremely convenient and i can already tell this will make many more things possible.

I think I might not be using my vocabulary to it's fullest extent.

2 - name of file (cannot contain curly brackets)

4 - Contents, must start and end with () parentheses

=K.text: Find where the version number is inside of SCS, split it, find what the version number is, change it by one, and replace the old version num with the new one.

Output: Terminal
Uses Variables: i, ii, iii, textbox

Counts the splits in an item.

Xi.text:
Makes a file:

Type is the filetype you want the file to be (Can be anything)

Contents is what you want to be inside the file

Locate is optional, but allows you to put the file in a directory other than the current working directory

WA.text:
Takes the input 

Output: iCmd, Terminal

Wj.text: Fun way to yell at people who mess with stuff

P=.text: Runs and documents the system boot and control set process

Pi.text:
Splits a given item

splitby is the token you want the script to split the item by

Modes: CMD, Limb, if you leave it empty, it will split into the Split list

VR.text: Takes in a user input specifically to input into the command interpreter
